creator: marklmi26-fbsd@yahoo.com
text:
(In reply to Dimitry Andric from comment #1)

As a test I tried chaining just sys.mk in my environment
to use += for CXXFLAGS instead of ?= and it started working:

# svnlite diff /usr/src/share/mk/sys.mk
Index: /usr/src/share/mk/sys.mk
===================================================================
--- /usr/src/share/mk/sys.mk	(revision 312009)
+++ /usr/src/share/mk/sys.mk	(working copy)
@@ -173,7 +173,7 @@
 .endif
 
 CXX		?=	c++
-CXXFLAGS	?=	${CFLAGS:N-std=*:N-Wnested-externs:N-W*-prototypes:N-Wno-pointer-sign:N-Wold-style-definition}
+CXXFLAGS	+=	${CFLAGS:N-std=*:N-Wnested-externs:N-W*-prototypes:N-Wno-pointer-sign:N-Wold-style-definition}
 IR_CXXFLAGS	?=	${STATIC_CXXFLAGS:N-O*} ${CXXFLAGS:N-O*}
 PO_CXXFLAGS	?=	${CXXFLAGS}
 

It appears that having SRCCONF's or SRC_ENV_CONF's referenced
file assign to (even with +=) CXXFLAGS ended up defining
CXXFLAGS before sys.mk executes and so the ?= notation for the
likes of CXXFLAGS is then ineffective in sys.mk.

This would not be limited to CXXFLAGS as far as ?= notation goes
in sys.mk vs. assignments in SRCCONF or SRC_ENV_CONF files.
